mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon as market participants price in strong economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds 4.5%, it signifies a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, which dampens economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS has been above 4.5% for at least two consecutive months over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of persistently low unemployment and strong payroll growth signals a tight labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and increased operating costs for companies, ultimately compressing profit margins and resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect of UNRATE below 4% and PAYEMS growth above 0.1% month-over-month does not correlate negatively with SPX 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a significant month-over-month increase in housing starts can signal a potential bottoming or stabilization in the housing market and broader economy, leading to a short-term positive market reaction in the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 0% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 5%
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of triggered instances when GDPC1 is contracting and HOUST shows a significant rebound.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production indicates robust manufacturing and supply-side activity, reflecting healthy economic growth that underpins corporate earnings and justifies higher equity valuations over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward returns of SPX do not exhibit a consistent positive correlation with INDPRO year-over-year growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in the Consumer Price Index, particularly after a period of elevated inflation, signals easing inflationary pressures, which may lead to less aggressive monetary policy and improved real corporate earnings, boosting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth falls by more than 0.5 percentage points from its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite a significant deceleration in CPIAUCSL growth.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and a decline in non-farm payrolls signifies a rapidly deteriorating labor market, indicating a broader economic slowdown that will negatively impact consumer demand and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS 3-month average change is negative
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the combined signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic growth, a sharp and unexpected decline in housing starts can signal an impending slowdown in consumer and investment demand, indicating a potential peak in the business cycle and leading to negative forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2% AND HOUST month-over-month change < -10%
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances during periods of strong GDP growth combined with a sharp drop in housing starts.
